description: Figure 12 presents finite-element simulation contour maps for Fe-30Mn-9Al-0.8C low-density steel subjected to warm multiaxial forging (MAF), comparing results after Pass 1, Pass 3, and Pass 5. The four rows correspond to (a) equivalent strain map (SDV1), (b) dislocation density map (SDV3, units m⁻², scale ~10¹⁵), (c) grain size distribution map (SDV6, units μm), and (d) flow stress map (SDV4, units MPa). For each variable, two sectional planes are shown per pass. Visual inspection reveals that equivalent strain, dislocation density, and flow stress progressively concentrate toward the specimen center, developing intense red-hot zones by Pass 5, while the grain size map exhibits an inverse pattern with finer grains in the center and coarser grains toward the outer regions. These trends demonstrate that increasing strain across the five MAF passes promotes dislocation accumulation and grain refinement, which in turn explains the marked rise in flow stress. The simulation results support the interpretation that suppressed dislocation climb and cross-slip at lower temperatures reduce dislocation mobility and annihilation at grain boundaries, thereby increasing both equivalent strain and dislocation density during warm MAF.
